project.name: ADAS Lane Keep Assist — ECU Platform v2.0

work_products.title: Safety Plan
work_products.content: This safety plan defines the functional safety management process for the LKA ECU project. It covers the safety lifecycle from concept through production release. Responsibilities are assigned to the Safety Manager, Project Lead, and System Architect. Milestones include HARA completion, TSC review, software integration, and safety validation. The plan is version-controlled and updated at each gate review.

work_products.title: Documentation Management Procedure
work_products.content: All functional safety documentation is managed under document control procedure DCP-101. Documents are version-controlled in Polarion with traceability to requirements. Access control, review workflows, and change history are maintained.

work_products.title: Item Definition — Lane Keep Assist
work_products.content: The Lane Keep Assist (LKA) system provides automated lateral vehicle control to maintain the vehicle within lane markings. The item includes a front-facing camera module, lane detection ECU, electric power steering (EPS) actuator, and CAN bus interfaces. External interfaces include driver steering wheel torque sensor, vehicle speed from ABS, and turn signal status. The system operates from -40C to +85C across active, standby, degraded, and off operating modes. Legal requirements include UNECE R79. Potential malfunctions include unintended steering torque, loss of lane detection, incorrect lane boundary interpretation, and failure to disengage on driver override.

work_products.title: Hazard Analysis and Risk Assessment
work_products.content: Hazard analysis per ISO 26262 Part 3. HE1: unintended steering into oncoming traffic (S3,E4,C2 → ASIL D). HE2: unintended steering off-road (S3,E4,C2 → ASIL D). HE3: loss of lane keeping during curve (S2,E3,C2 → ASIL B). HE4: failure to disengage (S1,E3,C1 → ASIL A). Operational situations include highway, urban, construction zones. Malfunctions mapped: camera failure, ECU fault, EPS stuck, CAN loss.

work_products.title: Safety Goals
work_products.content: SG1: LKA shall not apply unintended steering torque toward oncoming traffic (ASIL D). Safe state: EPS torque = 0 Nm. FTTI: 100ms. SG2: LKA shall not apply unintended steering torque toward road edge (ASIL D). Safe state: EPS torque = 0 Nm. FTTI: 100ms. SG3: LKA shall maintain lane keeping or degrade gracefully (ASIL B). Each SG documented with hazardous event, ASIL, rationale, assumptions.

work_products.title: Functional Safety Concept
work_products.content: FSR1: Limit torque overlay to ±3 Nm (ASIL D). FSR2: Independent monitoring detect torque deviations >0.5 Nm within 50ms (ASIL D). FSR3: Driver override detection within 20ms at >4 Nm torque (ASIL D). FSR4: Graceful degradation if camera lost (ASIL B). FSRs allocated to camera, ECU, EPS, safety monitor.

work_products.title: Technical Safety Concept
work_products.content: TSR1: EPS torque hardware-limited ±3 Nm by current limiter (ASIL D). TSR2: Redundant torque sensor with 50ms response (ASIL D). TSR3: Steering wheel torque threshold 4 Nm, 20ms latency (ASIL D). TSR4: Camera fallback to reduced-confidence mode (ASIL B). Requirements unambiguous, verifiable, traceable.

work_products.title: System Architecture
work_products.content: Components: Camera Module, Lane Detection ECU, EPS Actuator with hardware current limiter, CAN bus CRC protection. Safety mechanisms: redundant torque sensor, independent watchdog, hardware torque limiter, CRC-protected CAN. Partitioning ensures ASIL D independence from QM.

work_products.title: Hardware Safety Requirements
work_products.content: HSR1: Current limiter restricts EPS motor current to safe envelope (ASIL D). HSR2: Redundant torque sensor electrically independent (ASIL D). HSR3: Watchdog resets ECU within 10ms of heartbeat failure. Dual-channel sensor, independent power domains, -40C to +125C qualification.

work_products.title: Software Safety Requirements
work_products.content: SSR1: Lane detection output within 20ms cycle (ASIL D). SSR2: Torque controller enforces ±3 Nm software limit (ASIL D). SSR3: Safety monitor independently verifies torque each cycle (ASIL D). SSR4: Diagnostics logs safety events with timestamps (ASIL B). Traceable to TSRs.
methods_used: Use of language subsets (e.g., MISRA C)

work_products.title: Software Architecture Document
work_products.content: 5 AUTOSAR SWCs: Lane Detection, Path Planning, Torque Controller, Safety Monitor, Diagnostics. Memory partitioning, OS protection, HSIS references. Interfaces and data flow defined.

work_products.title: Verification and Change Management
work_products.content: Requirements-based testing covers SSRs and TSRs. Change management includes safety impact analysis. RTM with bidirectional traceability from SG to test cases in Polarion.
methods_used: Use of a requirements traceability matrix (RTM)
Bidirectional linking from SG to test cases

work_products.title: FMEA and Safety Analysis
work_products.content: System-level FMEA covering all functional blocks. Failure modes for camera, ECU, EPS, CAN. Inductive (FMEA) + deductive (FTA) analysis. Fault injection planned for ASIL D. Diagnostic coverage confirmed. Results feed safety case.
methods_used: Mapping findings to safety case argument structure
